title: Title and descriptive metadata

publication.changeSummary: Technology-specific HTML: Semantic and Accessible Web Documents draft for Title and descriptive metadata; independent standards, browser, pedagogy, and accessibility review required. Beginner-quality contract 1.0.0 applied deterministically from existing source-linked draft facts; publication stage and reviewer state are unchanged.

sources.locator: HTML Living Standard; living standard accessed 2026-07; provisions applicable to Title and descriptive metadata
sources.claim: A unique concise title identifies the page and current task; a meta description can summarize content for search presentation but is not visible page labeling.

sources.locator: MDN HTML Guide; accessed 2026-07; provisions applicable to Title and descriptive metadata
sources.claim: A unique concise title identifies the page and current task; a meta description can summarize content for search presentation but is not visible page labeling.

canonicalExplanation.summary: A unique concise title identifies the page and current task; a meta description can summarize content for search presentation but is not visible page labeling.

sections.heading: Start here
sections.body: Start with this case: The lesson page title reads Variables in C - Codestead rather than the same Home title used everywhere. Your goal is to author metadata for a small multi-page site without promising ranking behavior. Work through the example one step at a time, then use the boundary to check your answer.

sections.heading: Before you begin
sections.body: Review these declared prerequisites first: Document foundations; Text and page semantics; Forms and native controls; Standards-mode document skeleton; Relative and absolute URLs. If one is unfamiliar, revisit it before continuing.

sections.heading: Technology rule
sections.body: A unique concise title identifies the page and current task; a meta description can summarize content for search presentation but is not visible page labeling. Apply this rule to the actual markup, stylesheet, JavaScript, or component state rather than memorizing its name.

sections.heading: Boundary and browser reality
sections.body: Search engines can ignore descriptions, title updates matter in client navigation, and duplicate titles make tabs and history ambiguous. This draft therefore rejects: The meta description is the page's accessible heading and replaces an h1.

includes: Provide unique page titles and appropriate descriptive, canonical and social-preview metadata.
Author metadata for a small multi-page site without promising ranking behavior.
excludes: Search engines can ignore descriptions, title updates matter in client navigation, and duplicate titles make tabs and history ambiguous.

outcomes: Author metadata for a small multi-page site without promising ranking behavior.

examples.title: Title and descriptive metadata: concrete case
examples.situation: The lesson page title reads Variables in C - Codestead rather than the same Home title used everywhere.
walkthrough: Step 1 — Inspect the exact artifact and starting state: The lesson page title reads Variables in C - Codestead rather than the same Home title used everywhere.
Step 2 — Use the core rule above to predict one observable result.
Step 3 — Verify the correction: Provide a unique document title and visible heading; metadata descriptions do not replace in-page structure.
examples.result: The result follows because Provide a unique document title and visible heading; metadata descriptions do not replace in-page structure.

examples.title: Title and descriptive metadata: boundary case
examples.situation: Search engines can ignore descriptions, title updates matter in client navigation, and duplicate titles make tabs and history ambiguous.
walkthrough: Step 1 — Start from the documented boundary: Search engines can ignore descriptions, title updates matter in client navigation, and duplicate titles make tabs and history ambiguous.
Step 2 — Use the core rule above to predict one observable result.
Step 3 — Reject the misconception: The meta description is the page's accessible heading and replaces an h1.
examples.result: The boundary requires this correction: Provide a unique document title and visible heading; metadata descriptions do not replace in-page structure.
